title: Calendar columns allow impossible period values at the database boundary
reasoning: The schema accepts contribution months outside 1-12 and family due days outside a valid calendar-day range. If a command, import, factory, AI/tool path, or future code path writes directly to these tables, invalid rows can be persisted and then missed or mishandled by monthly reporting and due-date generation logic that assumes valid month and day values.
recommendation: Add database-level check constraints for valid month and due_day ranges, and keep request/command validation aligned. For an already-deployed database, add a forward migration that first audits or normalizes existing invalid rows before adding constraints.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature declares no linked tests, and these migrations only define column types; there is no included schema test asserting the database rejects invalid calendar values.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a migration/schema test that attempts to insert contributions with month 0 and 13, and families with due_day 0 and 32, then asserts each insert is rejected by the database.
minimumFixScope: A forward migration adding check constraints to contributions.month and families.due_day, plus validation on non-HTTP creation paths that can create these records.
